title: Sneeupoeding
excerpt: I'm going to say it shamelessly: getting to cook for Peter Veldsman was probably the greatest honour that ever came my way. I didn't know how to make sneeupoeding and he showed me. From now on it's part of my desserts.
html: I'm going to say it shamelessly: getting to cook for Peter Veldsman was probably the greatest honour that ever came my way.  I didn't know how to make sneeupoeding and he showed me.  From now on it's part of my desserts.

items: 2 egg whites (keep the yolks for the homemade custard)

steps: Dissolve the gelatine in the cold water.
Pour the boiling water, castor sugar, lemon juice and lemon zest into a pot and stir until the sugar has dissolved.
Stir in the gelatine and mix well.
Pour everything through a sieve and set aside to cool.
When you notice it starting to set, whisk it quickly until it starts to go frothy.
Whisk the egg whites stiff in a clean glass bowl.
Rinse out a nice serving bowl with cold water and pour the pudding into the bowl.
